compiled_state.title: Create SC coverage prompt files for haiku, sonnet, and opus tiers with input/output contract schemas
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## What
Create 3 prompt files defining the input/output contracts for each tier of the SC coverage gate. Then write a structural boundary test that verifies each file has the required sections.

## File Impact
- CREATE: `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/sc-coverage-haiku.md`
- CREATE: `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/sc-coverage-sonnet.md`
- CREATE: `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/sc-coverage-opus.md`
- CREATE: `tests/skills/test-sprint-sc-coverage-prompts.sh` (structural boundary test)
- EDIT: `.test-index` (add mapping + RED marker)

## Acceptance Criteria

### Structural test (RED first, then GREEN after prompt files are created)
Create `tests/skills/test-sprint-sc-coverage-prompts.sh` first and verify it fails (RED), then create the prompt files and verify the test passes (GREEN).

Test checks for each prompt file:
- File exists
- Contains an input schema section (## Input)
- Contains an output schema section (## Output or ## Output Schema)
- Contains JSON schema fields (sc_id or sc_text, verdict, etc.)
- Tier-specific: haiku file contains 'citation' or 'cite'; sonnet file contains 'UNSURE'; opus file contains 'COVERED-preferred' or 'tie-break' or 'ambiguous'

`.test-index` changes:
- Add: `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/sc-coverage-haiku.md: test-sprint-sc-coverage-prompts.sh`
- Add: `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/sc-coverage-sonnet.md: test-sprint-sc-coverage-prompts.sh`
- Add: `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/sc-coverage-opus.md: test-sprint-sc-coverage-prompts.sh`
- Add RED marker: `# [test_sc_coverage_prompts]`

### sc-coverage-haiku.md
Input schema:
- epic_sc_list: array of {sc_id, sc_text}
- children: array of {child_id, child_title, child_description}

Output schema (JSON):
- results: array of {sc_id, verdict: "COVERED" | "ESCALATE", covering_child_id: string | null, citation_reason: string | null}
- COVERED requires a non-null covering_child_id naming the specific child
- ESCALATE means the haiku agent cannot cite evidence

Behavioral instruction: cite only SCs you can validate by naming a specific covering child. If in doubt, escalate.

### sc-coverage-sonnet.md
Input schema:
- sc_list: array of {sc_id, sc_text} (original epic SC text only — no haiku verdicts)
- children: array of {child_id, child_title, child_description}

Output schema (JSON):
- results: array of {sc_id, verdict: "COVERED" | "MISSING" | "UNSURE", reasoning: string}

Behavioral instruction: evaluate each SC independently with no knowledge of haiku's prior escalation. Return COVERED if clear evidence exists, MISSING if no child addresses it, UNSURE if intent is ambiguous.

### sc-coverage-opus.md
Input schema:
- unsure_scs: array of {sc_id, sc_text} (SCs sonnet marked UNSURE only)
- children: array of {child_id, child_title, child_description}

Output schema (JSON):
- results: array of {sc_id, verdict: "COVERED" | "MISSING"}

Behavioral instruction: COVERED-preferred tie-breaking. For each SC, default to COVERED when the intent is plausible given the children descriptions. Return MISSING only when no plausible interpretation leads to coverage.

## testing_mode
RED — structural boundary test written before prompt files exist

## Done Definition
- Structural test exists, is executable, and passes GREEN after prompt files are created
- All 3 prompt files exist with correct input/output schemas
- .test-index entries added

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6 - All 3 prompt files created, structural test passing GREEN, .test-index updated
comments.author: Test
